WTI.data_anomaly: fast_info prev_close $91.72 (+1.5%) vs history prev_close $90.32 (+3.0%). Using fast_info as headline; history used for technicals only.

macro_summary: Markets sold off sharply Thursday as the Iran-Hormuz crisis entered day 18 with no resolution, combining with persistent Fed hawkish pause pressure to push SPY down 1.57% to $645.09 and QQQ down 2.18% to $573.79. The dominant new development per StockStory is Google's TurboQuant algorithm announcement, which threatens to reduce memory requirements for AI models — this single catalyst drove outsized selling in semiconductors, with MU down 5.56%, AMD down 7.34%, and SOXX collapsing 4.49% to $328.85. Per Yahoo Finance, Dow futures are rising overnight as Trump delayed military action against Iranian energy infrastructure, offering some relief, but the structural macro backdrop (VIX surging 8.24% to 27.44, US10Y rising to 4.42%) remains hostile to rate-sensitive tech names.
commodities_note: WTI data shows a conflict between sources: fast_info reports a prior close of $91.72 (+1.47% to $93.07), while history data implies a prior close of $90.32 (+3.0%). Using fast_info as the headline figure per pipeline convention; the 5-day trend is more reliable than the single-session move. Oil remains elevated with the Hormuz crisis structural premium intact — per Wood Mackenzie, prices could exceed $100/bbl if tanker flows are not quickly restored. Gold fell 1.97% to $4,421.30, pulling back from elevated levels, potentially on Trump's Iran delay news reducing immediate safe-haven demand. The Dollar Index (DXY) ticked up 0.27% to 99.86, reflecting the rate premium and flight to quality dynamic. GLD at current levels remains far above its 50-day average — no attractive entry exists despite the pullback.

D05_SI.signal_rationale:
Signal unchanged at HOLD. DBS sits 0.54% below its 50-day average of SGD 57.47 with RSI at 53.5 — technically neutral with no compelling entry dislocation. The R:R looks optically attractive at 6.7:1 (invalidation SGD 57.00, nearest support, with a tight 0.3% stop), but this tight stop is a structural concern: the SGD 57.00 level is only 0.3% away, meaning normal intraday volatility could trigger it before the thesis is tested. Use SGD 52.45 (the 200-day average, 8.2% below) as the mental stop for actual position sizing — that is the meaningful thesis test. NIM compression entering its 17th session with US10Y at 4.42% (moving further from the 4.20% reset threshold), and the dividend yield of 5.36% at 2.36x book provides the fundamental floor for patience. Analyst consensus expects earnings to decline 8.8% — a modest drag, but not thesis-breaking at 13.8x forward PE, which is in line with the Singapore cluster median.

Bottom line: DBS is a solid yield name at a fair price, but the macro headwind (high rates compressing bank margins) hasn't lifted. Wait for US10Y to break below 4.20% or a meaningful pullback toward SGD 52-53 before entering.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

O39_SI.signal_rationale:
Signal unchanged at WATCH. OCBC has climbed 2.24% above its rising 50-day average of SGD 21.13 with RSI at 59.3 — the cleanest technical setup in the Singapore cluster. However, the R:R is distorted: the ratio is mathematically extreme (0.5:1) because resistance at SGD 21.81 is only 1.0% away while the SMA50 invalidation sits 2.2% below. This means the near-term risk/reward doesn't support entry here — reward is capped at the next resistance while downside is more than double that. The PEG of 4.49 with only 2.7% earnings growth is elevated; OCBC is not cheap on a growth-adjusted basis though the 3.9% dividend yield provides income support. Volume has been below average throughout this rally — no institutional confirmation of the move. Structural NIM compression persists with US10Y at 4.42%, and at SGD 21.81 resistance sits just 0.3 SGD away.

Bottom line: OCBC is the best chart in the Singapore cluster but the upside to resistance is too thin to justify entry here. Wait for a pullback toward SGD 21.13 (50-day average) on above-average volume, or a breakout above SGD 21.81 with volume confirmation.

U11_SI.signal_rationale:
Signal changed from HOLD to CAUTION — UOB slipped 1.97% below its 50-day average of SGD 37.42 with analyst consensus expecting a 6.7% earnings decline, triggering the base scorer caution flag. Per Simply Wall St., UOB priced a CNY 5 billion fixed income offering including Eurobond and Panda bond tranches and completed redemption of previously issued notes — a liability management move that simplifies the debt profile but signals the bank is actively managing funding costs in a high-rate environment. R:R is unfavourable at 0.6:1: upside to resistance at SGD 37.40 is only 2.0% while invalidation at the 200-day average (SGD 35.55) is 3.1% below. At 10.1x forward PE (a 26.8% discount to the cluster median of 13.8x), the valuation is genuinely cheap, but the PEG of 2.34 with negative revenue growth (-1.8%) means the discount is warranted — growth is not there to justify expansion. The SMA50 is rising, which limits downside, but price needs to reclaim SGD 37.42 with volume before reconsidering.

Bottom line: UOB looks cheap on PE but that's because growth is declining. The bond issuance shows management is focused on balance sheet rather than earnings acceleration right now. Wait for SGD 37.42 reclaim with volume.

C6L_SI.signal_rationale:
Signal unchanged at CAUTION. Singapore Airlines sits essentially at its 50-day average of SGD 6.65 (0.17% below) with RSI at 49.5 — technically neutral, but the R:R is distorted and unfavourable: the ratio is 0.1:1 because resistance at SGD 6.65 is only 0.2% away while the 200-day average invalidation (SGD 6.55) sits 1.4% below. Tight stop warning: with invalidation only 1.4% away, normal intraday volatility may trigger it before the thesis is tested — use SGD 6.34 (4.5% below, structural support) as the actual mental stop for sizing. The dominant concern remains the Hormuz crisis now in day 18: elevated oil (WTI $93.07) directly compresses airline margins, and C6L's 64.3% expected earnings decline is a material fundamental concern that warrants caution regardless of technical positioning. At 17.4x forward PE (26.1% premium to the Singapore cluster median of 13.8x), you're paying above-average for a name with shrinking earnings. FCF yield of 8.27% is the only compelling metric.

Bottom line: SIA is fundamentally challenged by oil at current levels, and the 64% expected earnings decline is not priced in at a cluster premium. Avoid until oil retreats or the Hormuz crisis resolves.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

Z74_SI.signal_rationale:
Signal unchanged at WATCH. SingTel has spent 39 sessions above its rising 50-day average of SGD 4.86 (currently 2.94% above) with RSI at 51.5 — a steady uptrend without overheating. However, R:R at this entry is only 1.1:1: upside to SGD 5.15 resistance is 3.0% while the SMA50 invalidation is 2.8% below. Valuation is the constraint: forward PE of 24.7x is 79% above the Singapore cluster median of 13.8x, and with revenue growth of only 0.9%, the PEG of 3.8 is expensive for what you're getting. The 5-day decline of 4.03% bears watching — if this is the start of a pullback toward the 50-day average, that would create a better entry with improved R:R. Volume remains below average, limiting conviction on either side.

Bottom line: SingTel's chart is constructive but valuation is stretched for a near-zero-growth telecom. More interesting as a pullback play near SGD 4.86. Watch for a test of the 50-day average with above-average volume holding as support.

NVDA.signal_rationale:
Signal unchanged at CAUTION. NVDA dropped 3.82% Thursday to $171.24, now 6.83% below its 50-day average of $183.79, as Google's TurboQuant algorithm announcement (per StockStory) spooked the market on reduced AI memory and compute requirements — a potential headwind to demand that warrants monitoring but is not yet a confirmed thesis break. Tight stop warning: invalidation sits at $169.81 (0.8% below current price) — normal intraday volatility may trigger it before the thesis is tested. Use $164.30 (4.1% below, structural support) as the actual mental stop for position sizing. The Blackwell Ultra ramp (Q2-Q3 per NVDA Q4 FY2026 earnings call) and Q1 FY2027 guidance of $78B remain the structural thesis, and PEG of 0.71 with 73.2% revenue growth is compelling at 15.4x forward PE — a discount to the semis cluster median. However, with price now below declining 50-day and 100-day averages, and RSI at 38.9, ACCUMULATE gate check: while caution_source is base_scorer, the rr_distorted flag is false but tight_invalidation is true and the 0.8% downside creates a near-meaningless stop — the R:R of 9.6:1 is mathematically suspect given the stop is effectively within noise. Wait for RSI to approach 30 or price to find support at $164.30 before reassessing entry.

Bottom line: NVDA is attractively valued for its growth rate, but the chart is broken below key averages and the Google TurboQuant news adds near-term uncertainty. Wait for $164-165 support to hold before entering — the thesis is intact but the entry isn't.

AMD.signal_rationale:
Signal changed from WATCH to CAUTION — AMD plunged 7.34% to $203.77 on above-average volume as the Google TurboQuant algorithm announcement (per StockStory) hit memory-intensive AI chip names hardest, with AMD specifically cited among the stocks that plummeted in the afternoon session. The confirmed Meta $100B+ GPU supply deal (per AMD IR press release, Feb 24, 2026) and Ryzen AI 400 series launch (per AMD MWC 2026 press release) remain valid structural catalysts, but the single-day move on meaningful volume constitutes a thesis risk that must be acknowledged. ACCUMULATE gate check: caution_source is base_scorer, but R:R ratio of 1.7:1 (invalidation at $194.77 200-day average, upside to $218.84 resistance) is below the 2.0 threshold required for ACCUMULATE. At 19.0x forward PE (in line with cluster median) with a PEG of 0.6 and 34.1% revenue growth, AMD is one of the most attractively valued names on the watchlist — the 217% expected earnings growth adds significant fundamental support. However, volume-confirmed selling (-7.34% on above-average volume) signals institutional distribution rather than retail panic, and the 200-day average at $194.77 is the critical support to watch.

Bottom line: AMD is genuinely cheap relative to its growth rate, but the market just voted against it on volume. Wait to see if $194.77 (200-day average) holds before considering entry — that's the line between a buying opportunity and a deeper correction.

INTC.entry_block: CAUTION: forward P/E of 44x (>40x). Requires explicit justification of why earnings growth supports this multiple — default to WATCH unless thesis is clearly articulated with specific catalysts and timeline.
INTC.signal_rationale:
Signal changed from WATCH to CAUTION — Intel dropped 6.17% to $44.10, falling 5.29% below its 50-day average of $46.56 as the broader semis rout (SOXX -4.49%) pulled it lower. The entry block remains active: at 44.5x forward PE with negative free cash flow yield (-2.03%) and negative revenue growth (-4.1%), the pipeline's CAUTION flag for the elevated multiple is well-founded. The 18A foundry roadmap and CHIPS Act onshoring thesis (per catalyst data) remain structurally intact, but there are no near-term earnings growth catalysts to justify the multiple — analyst consensus has no earnings growth estimate available. R:R of 1.9:1 (invalidation at $42.58 support, upside to $46.97 resistance) is just below the ACCUMULATE threshold of 2.0, and the negative FCF yield makes the on-deck designation unjustifiable. Structural support at $34.82 (21.0% below) is the meaningful downside reference if $42.58 breaks. Intel earnings on April 24 (28 days away) is the nearest catalyst.

Bottom line: Intel's turnaround story is real but long-dated, and you're paying 44x forward earnings for a company with no current earnings growth and burning cash. Avoid until the 18A node generates visible revenue, or wait for the April 24 earnings print.

ASML.signal_rationale:
Signal changed from HOLD to CAUTION — ASML fell 4.83% to $1,329.50, now 4.76% below its 50-day average of $1,395.99, as the semis rout extended to equipment names despite ASML's fundamental insulation from the Google TurboQuant news. Per Investor's Business Daily, an analyst named ASML a 'top pick' as memory-chip makers upgrade gear, and per 24/7 Wall St., Bernstein raised its price target to $1,971 from $1,911 with an Outperform rating — bullish institutional conviction. However, R:R is unfavourable: upside of 5.9% to $1,407.54 resistance against invalidation 22.0% below at the 200-day average ($1,036.68) yields 0.3:1. At 30.6x forward PE with a PEG of 2.2, ASML trades at a 61.1% premium to the semis cluster median of 19.0x — expensive relative to peers, though monopoly EUV status justifies some premium. The High-NA EUV ramp for Intel 14A (H2 2026, per ASML Q4 2025 earnings call) is the structural driver. ASML earnings on April 15 (19 days away) is a critical near-term binary event.

Bottom line: ASML is the irreplaceable tool in chip manufacturing and the Bernstein target of $1,971 suggests significant upside — but wait for April 15 earnings before entering, as the print will confirm or deny whether the EUV ramp is on track.

MU.signal_rationale:
Signal unchanged at CAUTION. Micron fell 5.56% to $355.46, extending the 5-day loss to 20% as Google's TurboQuant algorithm — which reportedly threatens to reduce memory requirements for AI models (per StockStory) — directly challenged the HBM demand thesis that drove the record Q2 FY2026 earnings (confirmed March 18, per MU earnings release). This is the specific ticker-level event that keeps MU on CAUTION despite compelling valuation: at 3.6x forward PE (81.1% below the semis cluster median of 19.0x), the stock prices in significant cycle risk, but the PEG of 0.26 and 756% earnings growth appear anomalous — forward PE and trailing PE diverging by 4.7x signals an expected earnings inflection, not a sustainable run-rate. The FCF yield of 0.72% is low for a capital-intensive business. R:R of 1.0:1 with derived quality (support/resistance estimated from SMA levels, not observed swing points) does not support ACCUMULATE. RSI at 37.2 is approaching the contrarian zone. If TurboQuant proves less impactful than feared, the recovery could be sharp — but that's a bet on an unconfirmed technology claim.

Bottom line: Micron is statistically cheap but the Google TurboQuant news is a direct threat to its AI memory growth story — and until that's resolved, the valuation discount is deserved. Wait for clarity on how much TurboQuant actually reduces HBM demand before entering.

AMZN.signal_rationale:
Signal unchanged at CAUTION. Amazon fell 1.60% to $207.54, sitting 4.77% below its 50-day average of $217.95 with both the 100-day ($225.99) and 200-day ($224.77) averages above the 50-day — a stacked bearish technical structure. R:R of 1.1:1 (invalidation at $196.93 support, 5.1% below; upside to $219.43 resistance, 5.7%) doesn't justify entry. The Prime shipping expansion to third-party websites (per TheStreet) is a modest commercial development; the $200B AI capex commitment (per AMZN Q4 2025 earnings call and 10-K) remains the dominant thesis variable, with FCF yield of 1.07% confirming that investors are betting on future cash generation, not current. The Washington State refrigerant fine exceeding $800,000 (per Simply Wall St.) is immaterial to the investment case. At 22.1x forward PE (in line with the BigTech cluster median) with 13.6% revenue growth and a PEG of 1.92, AMZN is fairly valued but not cheap enough to buy broken technicals.

Bottom line: Amazon's AWS and AI story is solid but the stock needs the 50-day average reclaimed before it's worth entering. The current setup — broken trend, mediocre R:R, high capex burning FCF — argues for patience.

GOOG.signal_rationale:
Signal changed from CAUTION to HOLD — Google's RSI at 27.6 has reached oversold territory, representing a meaningful technical shift from yesterday's CAUTION, and the 5-day decline of 8.17% on above-average volume has now reset the entry calculus. The irony is significant: Google's own TurboQuant algorithm announcement (which hammered semis Thursday) is the proximate cause of GOOG's own selloff, per Yahoo Finance Video — the market apparently pricing in lower AI infrastructure spending that also reduces Google's cloud revenue. This is a legitimate concern, but at 20.9x forward PE (5.4% below the BigTech cluster median of 22.1x) with 18.0% revenue growth and 31.1% expected earnings growth, the fundamental case is increasingly compelling as price approaches the 200-day average of $262.48. R:R of 0.6:1 (upside 3.8% to $291.53 resistance, downside 6.5% to 200-day invalidation) is currently unfavourable, which prevents a WATCH or higher signal. Volume-confirmed selling over 5 sessions (-8.17%) reflects institutional distribution, not just noise. GOOG and INTC earnings on April 24 will be critical signal resets.

Bottom line: Google is getting genuinely cheap relative to its growth rate, and RSI below 30 historically marks the zone where patient entries pay off. But the R:R doesn't support entry here — wait for the $262-263 zone (200-day average) or a bounce with volume from current levels above that support.

LITE.entry_block: BLOCKED: +20.8% above 50-day SMA (>20% extreme extension). No trend exception applies at this level — entry requires a meaningful pullback regardless of trend duration.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

LITE.signal_rationale:
Signal unchanged at CAUTION. Lumentum plunged 11.6% to $688.80 in a single session but remains 20.77% above its 50-day average of $570.33 — the hard block remains firmly in place (BLOCKED: extreme extension >20%). The pipeline block cannot be overridden at this level regardless of fundamentals. The NVDA $2B strategic investment (confirmed per NVIDIA investor.nvidia.com press release, March 2, 2026) and S&P 500 inclusion (March 23, per 24/7 Wall St.) are confirmed structural catalysts, but the PEG of 0.14 with 65.5% revenue growth appears anomalous given 46x forward PE and negative FCF yield (-0.04%) — do not rely on the PEG figure as a primary valuation argument. The trailing PE of 200.2x reflects a company still growing into its multiple. Invalidation at $570.33 (50-day average) represents 17.2% downside from current levels.

Bottom line: Lumentum is a genuinely exciting AI optics story with confirmed NVIDIA backing, but you're buying at 21% above the 50-day average after a 989% one-year run. Wait for a meaningful pullback toward $528-570 before considering entry.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

COHR.signal_rationale:
Signal changed from HOLD to WATCH — Coherent pulled back 9.25% to $243.29 but remains 3.32% above its rising 50-day average of $235.48, and with 140 sessions above the SMA50 with it rising, this is a structural uptrend that a single bad session doesn't invalidate. The NVDA $2B strategic investment (confirmed per NVIDIA newsroom press release, March 2, 2026) and S&P 500 inclusion are confirmed catalysts. R:R of 0.5:1 (upside 1.6% to $247.15 resistance, downside 3.2% to $235.48 SMA50 invalidation) is distorted by how close resistance is — not a setup for entry today. Per Simply Wall St., Coherent's assessed fair value shifted from $273.11 to $284.25, with Street research linking it more closely to AI infrastructure spending. The PEG of 0.05 with 17.5% revenue growth and 73% earnings growth appears anomalous against 32.8x forward PE — flag as 'reported' and do not cite as a primary valuation argument. FCF yield of 0.29% means the thesis is entirely growth-dependent.

Bottom line: Coherent is a legitimate AI optics play with confirmed NVIDIA investment, but wait for the pullback to test the 50-day average ($235.48) with volume holding as support before entering — that's where the R:R becomes attractive.

XLE.entry_block: BLOCKED: +13.2% above 50-day SMA (>5% hard block). Sustained trend exception not met: RSI 80 (>=55).

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

XLE.signal_rationale:
Signal changed from CAUTION (caution_trim) to CAUTION — XLE rose 1.43% to $61.52 as the Hormuz crisis supports oil prices, but the hard entry block remains: 13.23% above the 50-day average of $54.33 with RSI at 80.3 (overbought). No entry is warranted at these levels regardless of the energy thesis. The pipeline block (BLOCKED: +13.2% above 50-day SMA, RSI 80) is non-negotiable. R:R of 0.4:1 with derived quality confirms the entry is unattractive. Per reporting from MT Newswires, energy stocks have been advancing for multiple sessions with the NYSE Energy Sector Index up 1.4-1.7%. Structural support sits at $46.32 (200-day average, 24.7% below), which gives a sense of how extended this move is. The energy rally is real, but chasing RSI 80 with a 13% extension above the 50-day is how investors get trapped at cycle tops. If WTI retreats toward $80 on Hormuz resolution, XLE could mean-revert sharply.

Bottom line: The oil story is real but XLE has priced in a lot of it already. At RSI 80 and 13% above its 50-day average, avoid new entry — wait for a pullback toward $54-55 (50-day average) if you want exposure.

PLTR.entry_block: CAUTION: forward P/E of 79x (>40x). Requires explicit justification of why earnings growth supports this multiple — default to WATCH unless thesis is clearly articulated with specific catalysts and timeline.
PLTR.signal_rationale:
Signal unchanged at CAUTION. Palantir fell 4.47% to $147.56, now 1.41% below its 50-day average of $149.68 with RSI at 46.7. The Golden Dome missile defense project — reported by Benzinga citing Reuters as Palantir reportedly collaborating with Anduril on Trump's $185 billion initiative — is a potentially massive catalyst, but sourced from unnamed sources and the companies did not immediately respond to comment requests, so treat as unconfirmed at this stage. The Army $10B IDIQ framework (indefinite delivery/indefinite quantity — not firm revenue) and the confirmed Navy $448M ShipOS deal (per DoD press release, per catalyst notes) are real but the IDIQ ceiling creates revenue uncertainty. At 79x forward PE (257.5% premium to BigTech cluster median of 22.1x) with PEG 2.98, this is extremely expensive — the entry block for forward PE >40x applies. R:R of 1.9:1 (invalidation at $126.30, 14.4% below; upside to $187.08, 26.8%) is just below the 2.0 ACCUMULATE threshold. The 647.6% expected earnings growth is the bull case, but FCF yield of 0.36% means nothing is in the bank yet.

Bottom line: Palantir's defense AI story is the most interesting narrative on the watchlist, but 79x forward earnings prices in perfection. The Golden Dome news is unconfirmed. Wait for confirmation of the contract and a reclaim of the 50-day average ($149.68) before entering.

VUAA_L.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. Would reconsider above $131.31 (50-day average) with volume confirmation and VIX dropping below 22.

singapore.summary: The Singapore cluster enters its 18th consecutive cautious session with no reset signal. US10Y at 4.42% is moving further from the 4.20% reset threshold, and the NIM compression thesis tightens further. OCBC (O39_SI) remains the relative outperformer with a rising 50-day average, but UOB's signal downgrade to CAUTION today marks the first intra-cluster deterioration this week.
singapore.key_development: UOB's CNY 5 billion bond issuance signals active liability management in a high-rate environment, confirming the NIM pressure is affecting funding strategy across the cluster.

semiconductors.summary: Google's TurboQuant algorithm announcement broke the semis cluster sharply, with SOXX collapsing 4.49% to $328.85 — AMD and Intel both downgraded to CAUTION today on volume-confirmed selling. The key divergence within the cluster: ASML (equipment monopoly, insulated from demand reduction algorithms) vs. MU/AMD (direct AI compute and memory demand names most exposed to TurboQuant fears). Until the actual impact of TurboQuant on HBM demand is quantified, the entire cluster stays defensive.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Deteriorating — TurboQuant threat to AI memory demand thesis
semiconductors.key_development: Google's TurboQuant algorithm, which reportedly reduces AI model memory requirements, triggered simultaneous 5-7% declines in MU and AMD on above-average volume — this is the most significant cluster-level thesis risk since the Hormuz crisis began.

big_tech.summary: BigTech cluster bifurcated: GOOG upgraded to HOLD as RSI hits 27.6 (oversold), while AMZN stays CAUTION and PLTR falls below its 50-day average. Google is both the cause (TurboQuant announcement) and a casualty of today's selloff — the algorithm that potentially reduces competitor AI spend also raises questions about Google's own cloud revenue trajectory.
big_tech.thesis_status: Under pressure — duration headwinds plus self-inflicted TurboQuant uncertainty
big_tech.key_development: Google's TurboQuant algorithm is the dominant catalyst of the day, creating a paradox where Google's own innovation is pricing down both competitors and its own stock simultaneously.

photonics.summary: Both LITE and COHR suffered double-digit drops today despite confirmed NVIDIA $2B investments in each (per NVIDIA newsroom and investor.nvidia.com press releases). The Google TurboQuant news indirectly threatens AI optics by raising questions about overall AI infrastructure capex intensity. COHR upgraded to WATCH as the pullback creates a better entry setup approaching the 50-day average.
photonics.thesis_status: Structurally intact — NVDA backing confirmed, but near-term noise elevated
photonics.key_development: LITE remains hard-blocked at 20.8% extension despite the 11.6% single-day drop, while COHR's pullback to 3.32% above its 50-day average brings it into the watch zone for patient accumulation.

energy.summary: XLE rose 1.43% as oil held above $93 with the Hormuz crisis structural premium intact, but RSI at 80.3 and 13.2% extension above the 50-day average make new entries unjustifiable at current levels. The sector is the only green cluster on the watchlist today, but it's overbought by any technical measure.
energy.thesis_status: Overbought — Hormuz premium intact but technically exhausted
energy.key_development: Trump's reported delay of military action against Iranian energy infrastructure (per Yahoo Finance) triggered overnight futures recovery, potentially capping the energy premium that has driven XLE to RSI 80.

index.summary: VUAA_L fell 1.29% to $125.54, now 4.39% below its 50-day average with RSI at 34.2 — approaching oversold territory but no reversal conviction. The broader SPY -1.57% and QQQ -2.18% declines confirm the index breakdown is accelerating, with VIX jumping 8.24% to 27.44.
index.key_development: VIX at 27.44 (up 8.24% in a single session) combined with SPY breaking to $645.09 suggests 6,500 support has now broken — the next meaningful support level to watch is the 200-day average.

interconnected.name: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing
interconnected.reason: AMD's confirmed Meta $100B+ GPU deal and NVDA's Blackwell Ultra ramp both flow through TSMC fabs — if Google TurboQuant reduces AI compute demand, TSMC advanced node utilisation is the first place it shows up.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if TSM US ADR (ticker: TSM) pulls back 8-10% from current levels on continued semis sector weakness — that would bring it closer to its 50-day average.

interconnected.name: Onto Innovation
interconnected.reason: Per 24/7 Wall St., Onto Innovation was cited as the next photonics-adjacent name to watch after LITE and COHR surged — directly linked to the AI optics infrastructure buildout narrative that NVDA's $2B investments in LITE and COHR validated.
interconnected.entry_note: Revisit after LITE and COHR stabilise above their respective 50-day averages — if the photonics sector consolidates rather than corrects, ONTO becomes the next-tier entry with a less extended chart.

interconnected.name: Broadcom
interconnected.reason: AVGO's custom AI ASIC chips (for Google, Meta, Apple) are directly competing with NVDA and AMD in the same hyperscaler capex budgets — the Google TurboQuant development that reduced AMD on volume today is also a read-through to custom silicon demand vs. merchant GPU demand.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if AVGO pulls back 8-12% from current levels on continued semis sector weakness, bringing it near its 50-day average — that's the level where the structural custom ASIC thesis becomes an attractive entry.

active_risks: Iran-Hormuz crisis day 18: Strait traffic at 2 vessels/day (vs. 138 baseline), WTI holding $93.07 with $100+ scenario intact
Google TurboQuant algorithm threatens AI memory demand thesis — direct hit to MU, AMD, NVDA thesis integrity
US-China tech restrictions escalating: proposed Entity List expansion to Huawei/YMTC subsidiaries could affect ASML export pipeline by June
Fed hawkish pause (3.50-3.75%, US10Y 4.42%) compressing mega-cap tech multiples — no rate cut expected until later 2026
geopolitical.market_impact: The Hormuz premium supports XLE above $61 (RSI 80 overbought) while simultaneously compressing C6L_SI margins — the oil spike is helping energy but hurting airlines and consumer names. The TurboQuant development landed hardest on MU (down 5.56% with 20% 5-day loss) and AMD (down 7.34% on above-average volume with a signal downgrade to CAUTION today), raising questions about whether the AI memory supercycle thesis is intact.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: Google announced TurboQuant algorithm reportedly reducing AI model memory requirements (per StockStory, March 26), triggering cluster-wide semis selloff; Trump reportedly delayed military action against Iranian energy infrastructure per Yahoo Finance, sending Dow futures higher overnight.
geopolitical.portfolio_action: Hold cash, no new entries across semis or BigTech until TurboQuant's actual memory demand impact is quantified and Hormuz resolution or escalation becomes clearer — the combination of a new AI demand threat and ongoing oil shock creates too much uncertainty for fresh positions.

events_this_week.event: CPI Report (March) — critical for Fed rate-cut probability reset; US10Y at 4.42% needs to see CPI soften meaningfully to relieve tech multiple compression
ASML Q1 2026 Earnings — could resolve the CAUTION signal if High-NA EUV customer ramp confirmation is provided; 19 days away
GOOG + INTC Earnings on same day — GOOG print will confirm or deny whether TurboQuant represents a real demand shift; INTC will signal 18A ramp progress
AMD Meta $100B+ GPU deal (MI450) — first 1GW shipments H2 2026 per AMD IR press release; could be the catalyst that reverses AMD's CAUTION signal
NVDA Blackwell Ultra (B300) ramp into volume production — per NVDA Q4 FY2026 earnings call; thesis confirmation event for the semis cluster
US Entity List expansion targeting Huawei/YMTC subsidiaries potentially in force — per Everstream, could affect ASML China export pipeline
